source_refs.html: /Users/toy/English/躺着学/躺着学阅读/8187_Passage 2 Left-handed or Right-handed.html

passages.title: Left-handed or Right-handed?
passages.source_html:
Left-handed or Right-handed? 


A 


The probability that two right-handed people would have a left-handed child is only about 9.5 percent. The chance rises to 19.5 percent if one parent is a lefty and 26 percent if both parents are left-handed: The preference, however, could also stem from an infant’s imitation of his parents. To test genetic influence, starting in the 1970s British biologist Marian Annett of the University of Leicester hypothesized that no single gene determines handedness. Rather, during fetal development, a certain molecular factor helps to strengthen the brain’s left hemisphere, which increases the probability that the right hand will be dominant because the left side of the brain controls the right side of the body, and vice versa. Among the minority of people who lack this factor, handedness develops entirely by chance. Research conducted on twins complicates the theory, however. One in five sets of identical twins involves one right-handed and one left-handed person, despite the fact that their genetic material is the same. Genes, therefore, are not solely responsible for handedness.


B 


The genetic theory is also undermined by results from Peter Hepper and his team at Queen’s University in Belfast, Ireland. In 2004 the psychologists used ultrasound to show that by the 15th week of pregnancy, fetuses already have a preference as to which thumb they suck. In most cases, the preference continued after birth. At 15 weeks, though, the brain does not yet have control over the body’s limbs. Hepper speculates that fetuses tend to prefer whichever side of the body is developing quicker and that their movements, in turn, influence the brain’s development. Whether this early preference is temporary or holds up throughout development and infancy is unknown. Genetic predetermination is also contradicted by the widespread observation that children do not settle on either their right or left hand until they are two or three years old. 


C 


But even if these correlations were true, they did not explain what actually causes left-handedness. Furthermore, specialization on either side of the body is common among animals. Cats will favor one paw over another when fishing toys out from under the couch. Horses stomp more frequently with one hoof than the other. Certain crabs motion predominantly with the left or right claw. In evolutionary terms, focusing power and dexterity in one limb is more efficient than having to train two, four or even eight limbs equally. Yet for most animals, the preference for one side or the other is seemingly random. The overwhelming dominance of the right hand is associated only with humans. That fact directs attention toward the brain’s two hemispheres and perhaps toward language. 


D 


Interest in hemispheres dates back to at least 1836. That year, at a medical conference, French physician Marc Dax reported on an unusual commonality among his patients. During his many years as a country doctor, Dax had encountered more than 40 men and women for whom speech was difficult, the result of some kind of brain damage. What was unique was that every individual suffered damage to the left side of the brain. At the conference, Dax elaborated on his theory, stating that each half of the brain was responsible for certain functions and that the left hemisphere controlled speech. Other experts showed little interest in the Frenchman’s ideas. Over time, however, scientists found more and more evidence of people experiencing speech difficulties following an injury to the left brain. Patients with damage to the right hemisphere most often displayed disruptions in perception or concentration. Major advancements in understanding the brain’s asymmetry were made in the 1960s as a result of so-called split-brain surgery, developed to help patients with epilepsy. During this operation, doctors severed the corpus callosum – the nerve bundle that connects the two hemispheres. The surgical cut also stopped almost all normal communication between the two hemispheres, which offered researchers the opportunity to investigate each side’s activity. 


E 


In 1949 neurosurgeon Juhn Wada devised the first test to provide access to the brain’s functional organization of language. By injecting an anesthetic into the right or left carotid artery, Wada temporarily paralyzed one side of a healthy brain, enabling him to more closely study the other side’s capabilities. Based on this approach, Brenda Milner and the late Theodore Rasmussen of the Montreal Neurological Institute published a major study in 1975 that confirmed the theory that country doctor Dax had formulated nearly 140 years earlier: in 96 percent of right-handed people, language is processed much more intensely in the left hemisphere. The correlation is not as clear in lefties, however. For two-thirds of them, the left hemisphere is still the most active language processor. But for the remaining third, either the right side is dominant or both sides work equally, controlling different language functions. That last statistic has slowed acceptance of the notion that the predominance of right-handedness is driven by left-hemisphere dominance in language processing. It is not at all clear why language control should somehow have dragged the control of body movement with it. Some experts think one reason the left hemisphere reigns over language is that the organs of speech processing – the larynx and tongue – are positioned on the body’s symmetry axis. Because these structures were centered, it may have been unclear, in evolutionary terms, which side of the brain should control them, and it seems unlikely that shared operation would result in smooth motor activity. Language and handedness could have developed preferentially for very different reasons as well. For example, some researchers, including evolutionary psychologist Michael C. Corballis of the University of Auckland in New Zealand, think that the origin of human speech lies in gestures. Gestures predated words and helped language emerge. If the left hemisphere began to dominate speech, it would have dominated gestures, too, and because the left brain controls the right side of the body, the right hand developed more strongly. 


F 


Perhaps we will know more soon. In the meantime, we can revel in what, if any, differences handedness brings to our human talents. Popular wisdom says right-handed, left-brained people excel at logical, analytical thinking. Left-handed, right-brained individuals are thought to possess more creative skills and maybe better at combining the functional features emergent on both sides of the brain. Yet some neuroscientists see such claims as pure speculation. Fewer scientists are ready to claim that left-handedness means greater creative potential. Yet lefties are prevalent among artists, composers and the generally acknowledged great political thinkers. Possibly if these individuals are among the lefties whose language abilities are evenly distributed between hemispheres, the intense interplay required could lead to unusual mental capabilities.


G 


Or perhaps some lefties become highly creative because they must be more clever to get by in our right-handed world. This battle, which begins during the very early stages of childhood, may lay the groundwork for exceptional achievements.

groups.title: Questions 14-26
groups.source_html:
Questions 14-18


The passage has seven sections A-G. Which section contains the following information? Write the correct letter A-G, in boxes 14-18 on your answer sheet. 


NB you may use any letter more than once.


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
14

14 The phenomenon of using one side of their body for animals
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：Furthermore, specialization on either side of the body is common among animals. Cats will favor one paw over another when fishing toys out from under the couch. Horses stomp more frequently with one hoof than the other. Certain crabs motion predominantly with the left or right claw. 

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问的是哪一段提到了动物使用单侧身体的现象。

定位关键信息：利用题干中的"animals"和"using one side of their body"这两个关键词定位到文章中的C段。

解题思路：在C段中，明确提到了动物使用身体单侧的现象，如把玩具从沙发下弄出来的时候，猫更偏向于用某只爪子，马匹用单蹄刨地的频率往往高于另一只蹄。某些螃蟹主要依靠左钳或右钳进行移动。这些例子直接对应了题目中询问的现象，因此C段包含了题目所要求的信息。
15

15 Statistics on the rate of one-handedness born 
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：The probability that two right-handed people would have a left-handed child is only about 9.5 percent. The chance rises to 19.5 percent if one parent is a lefty and 26 percent if both parents are left-handed.  

对应段落：A段  

审题：题目问的是哪一段提到了出生就带有偏手性的统计数据。  

定位关键信息：通过题干中的“Statistics on the rate of one-handedness”和“born”可以定位到A段，该段提到了不同父母组合下生出左撇子孩子的概率。  

解题思路：A段明确给出了右撇子父母生出左撇子孩子的概率为9.5%，如果其中一位父母是左撇子，概率上升到19.5%，如果父母都是左撇子，概率为26%。这些数据直接对应了题目中偏手性的统计数据，因此答案为A。
16

16 The age when the preference for using one hand is fixed
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：Genetic predetermination is also contradicted by the widespread observation that children do not settle on either their right or left hand until they are two or three years old. 

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是哪一段提到了惯用手偏好形成的年龄。

定位关键信息：根据题目的“age when the preference for using one hand is fixed”定位到B段末句“children do not settle on either their right or left hand until they are two or three years old”，明确指出儿童在两到三岁之间才形成惯用手的偏好。

解题思路：原文明确提到“children do not settle on either their right or left hand until they are two or three years old”，即孩子们在两三岁之前，都无法确定自己惯用的手是右手还是左手，因此答案为B段。
17

17 great talents of occupations in the left-handed population
✅ 正确答案：F

原文答案句：Yet lefties are prevalent among artists, composers and the generally acknowledged great political thinkers.

对应段落：F段  

审题：题目问的是哪一段提到了左撇子人群中出现了杰出人才。  

定位关键信息：根据题干中的"great talents of occupations in the left-handed population"（左撇子人群中出现杰出人才），可以定位到F段。  

解题思路：F段中提到很多艺术家、作曲家和政治思想家都是左撇子，与题干中的"great talents of occupations"对应，因此答案为F。
18

18 The earliest record of researching hemisphere’s function
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：Interest in hemispheres dates back to at least 1836. That year, at a medical conference, French physician Marc Dax reported on an unusual commonality among his patients.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问最早研究大脑半球功能的记录出现在哪个段落。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"the earliest record"和"researching hemisphere’s function"定位到文章中提到最早研究大脑半球功能的段落。

解题思路：D段的第一句话明确提到"Interest in hemispheres dates back to at least 1836"，说明最早对大脑半球功能的研究记录可以追溯到1836年。接下来的句子详细描述了法国医生Marc Dax在1836年的一次医学会议上报告了他对患者的研究，这些患者都有大脑左半球的损伤导致语言困难。因此，D段包含了关于最早研究大脑半球功能的记录。

Questions 19-22


Look at the following researchers and the list of findings below. March each researcher with the correct finding.


A Brenda Milner 

B Marian Annett 

C Peter Hepper 

D Michale Corballis 


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
19

19 Ancient language evolution is connected to body gesture and therefore influences handedness.
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：For example, some researchers, including evolutionary psychologist Michael C. Corballis of the University of Auckland in New Zealand, think that the origin of human speech lies in gestures. Gestures predated words and helped language emerge. If the left hemisphere began to dominate speech, it would have dominated gestures, too, and because the left brain controls the right side of the body, the right hand developed more strongly. 

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是哪位研究者认为古代语言进化与身体手势有关，因此影响惯用手偏好。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"ancient language evolution"、"body gesture"和"handedness"来定位答案在文章中的句子。

解题思路：根据原文答案句中的内容，Michael Corballis认为手势先于语言出现，并且帮助语言的形成。如果左脑开始主导语言，那么它也会主导手势，由于左脑控制身体的右侧，右手就会发展得更强势。这与题目中描述的观点相吻合，因此答案为D。
20

20 A child handedness is not determined by just biological factors.
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：Research conducted on twins complicates the theory, however. One in five sets of identical twins involves one right-handed and one left-handed person, despite the fact that their genetic material is the same. Genes, therefore, are not solely responsible for handedness.

对应段落：A段

审题：使用 "child handedness", "not determined by just biological factors" 定位到文中讨论用手习惯成因且涉及非生物因素的句子。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词"child handedness"和"not determined by just biological factors"可以定位到B段，该段提到了Peter Hepper和他的团队的研究结果，表明孩子的用手习惯不仅仅由生物因素决定。

解题思路：题目陈述的核心是“用手习惯并非仅由生物因素决定”。在段落A中，Marian Annett提出了关于用手习惯的基因假说，但随后原文用双胞胎研究对此进行了修正和复杂化：尽管同卵双胞胎基因相同，但仍有五分之一的情况是一个用右手，一个用左手。由此得出的结论是“基因并非用手习惯的唯一决定因素”。这直接支持了题目观点，即生物因素（基因）并非唯一决定因素。虽然这个结论是基于对Annett理论的补充和修正，但它是在介绍和讨论Annett理论的部分提出的，因此这个研究发现对应的是研究者B (Marian Annett)。
21

21 Language process is generally undergoing in the left hemisphere of the brain. 
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：Based on this approach, Brenda Milner and the late Theodore Rasmussen of the Montreal Neurological Institute published a major study in 1975 that confirmed the theory that country doctor Dax had formulated nearly 140 years earlier: in 96 percent of right-handed people, language is processed much more intensely in the left hemisphere.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是哪位研究者发现了语言处理通常发生在左脑。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的"Language process"和"left hemisphere"可以定位到E段中关于语言处理和大脑半球的信息。

解题思路：根据原文中的句子，Brenda Milner和Theodore Rasmussen在1975年发表了一项重要研究，证实了Dax医生近140年前提出的理论：在96%的右撇子中，语言处理在左脑中更为强烈。因此，可以确定Brenda Milner和Theodore Rasmussen是发现语言处理通常发生在左脑半球的研究者。
22

22 The rate of development of one side of the body has an influence on hemisphere preference in the fetus.
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：In 2004 the psychologists used ultrasound to show that by the 15th week of pregnancy, fetuses already have a preference as to which thumb they suck. In most cases, the preference continued after birth. At 15 weeks, though, the brain does not yet have control over the body’s limbs. Hepper speculates that fetuses tend to prefer whichever side of the body is developing quicker and that their movements, in turn, influence the brain’s development. 

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目问的是哪位研究人员的研究发现是“身体单侧的发展速度会影响胎儿的大脑半球偏好”。  

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词“rate of development of one side of the body”和“hemisphere preference in the fetus”定位到B段，找到Hepper的研究内容。  

解题思路：B段提到Hepper的研究发现，胎儿在15周时已经表现出吮吸拇指的偏好，但此时大脑尚未控制肢体。Hepper推测胎儿倾向于更喜欢发育更快的身体一侧，而这种运动反过来会影响大脑的发展。这与题目中“身体单侧的发展速度会影响胎儿的大脑半球偏好”一致，因此答案为C。

Questions 23-26


Do the following statements agree with the information given in the passage? In boxes 23-26 on your answer sheet, write YES, if the statement is true, NO if the statement is false, NOT GIVEN if the information is not givern in the passage.


23

The study of twins shows that genetic determination is not the only factor for left Handedness.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：One in five sets of identical twins involves one right-handed and one left-handed person, despite the fact that their genetic material is the same. Genes, therefore, are not solely responsible for handedness.

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问的是“对双胞胎的研究表明，基因决定不是左撇子的唯一因素”这一说法是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词“twins”和“genetic determination”在文章中定位，可以找到A段提到对双胞胎的研究结果。

解题思路：原文明确指出，即使双胞胎的基因相同，仍有五分之一的同卵双胞胎中一个是右撇子，一个是左撇子，并且明确说明基因并非决定惯用手的唯一决定因素。这与题目中的陈述完全一致，因此选择A（True）。


24

The number of men with left-handedness is more than that of women.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：无（原文中没有直接提及男女左撇子数量对比的信息）

对应段落：无（原文各段均未明确比较男女左撇子数量）

审题：题目询问“男性左撇子的数量是否多于女性”，需要根据原文信息判断这一陈述是否正确、错误或未提及。

定位关键信息：题目中的核心词汇是“men”（男性）、“women”（女性）和“left-handedness”（左撇子），需要寻找原文中是否比较了这两类人群的左撇子数量。

解题思路：通读全文后，发现原文中完全没有提及男性或女性左撇子的数量对比。文章讨论了左撇子的遗传、大脑半球功能、进化原因等内容，但未涉及性别差异。因此，题目中的信息在原文中未给出，应选择“NOT GIVEN”。


25

Marc Dax’s report was widely recognized in his time.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：Other experts showed little interest in the Frenchman’s ideas.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是“Marc Dax的报告在他那个时代是否被广泛认可”。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的“Marc Dax”和“his time”可以定位到D段，该段提到Marc Dax在1836年提出理论，但其他专家对他的想法兴趣不大。

解题思路：原文明确提到“Other experts showed little interest in the Frenchman’s ideas.”（其他专家对他的想法兴趣不大），说明他的报告并没有被广泛认可，因此题目中的说法是错误的。


26

Juhn Wada based his findings on his research of people with language problems.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：In 1949 neurosurgeon Juhn Wada devised the first test to provide access to the brain’s functional organization of language. By injecting an anesthetic into the right or left carotid artery, Wada temporarily paralyzed one side of a healthy brain, enabling him to more closely study the other side’s capabilities.  

对应段落：E段  

审题：题目问Juhn Wada的研究是否基于有语言障碍的人。  

定位关键信息：题目中的"Juhn Wada"和"research of people with language problems"是定位关键词。在E段中找到Juhn Wada的描述，发现他通过注射麻醉剂使健康大脑的一侧暂时瘫痪，研究另一侧的能力。  

解题思路：原文明确指出Juhn Wada的研究对象是"healthy brain"（大脑健康）的人，但研究对象是否有语言障碍，文章并未提到，因此题目说法属于未提及的信息。答案为C。
